questions.question:
In an election, the share of valid votes received by the four candidates A, B, C and D is represented by the pie chart shown. The total number of votes cast in the election were 1,15,000, out of which 5,000 were invalid. Based on the data provided, the total number of valid votes received by the candidates B and C is:

Pie chart: A = 40%, B = 25%, C = 20%, D = 15%

A. 45,000
B. 49,500
C. 51,750
D. 54,000
questions.to_find: total valid votes received by candidates B and C together

understand.plain: A pie chart splits 100% of valid votes among four candidates. We are given the total votes cast (including invalid ones) and need to first find the valid vote count, then apply the pie-chart percentages for B and C to get their combined tally.

keywords.explain: Votes that are counted in the election result, obtained by subtracting invalid votes from total votes cast.
Each candidate's slice represents their share of the total valid votes, so candidate B at 25% means B received 25% of all valid votes.
To find the total votes for two candidates together, add their individual percentages first, then multiply by the valid vote count.
keywords.example: B + C share = 25% + 20% = 45% of valid votes

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 400 300' width='400' height='300'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='400' height='300' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)' rx='10'/><circle cx='150' cy='150' r='110' fill='none' stroke='var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9)' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M150,150 L150,40 A110,110 0 0,1 245,205 Z' fill='#E8C4A0'/><path d='M150,150 L245,205 A110,110 0 0,1 81,229 Z' class='hi'/><path d='M150,150 L81,229 A110,110 0 0,1 71,97 Z' fill='#A0C8E8'/><path d='M150,150 L71,97 A110,110 0 0,1 150,40 Z' fill='#A0E8B4'/><text x='175' y='85' class='tag ink'>A 40%</text><text x='240' y='200' class='tag ink'>B 25%</text><text x='50' y='265' class='tag ink'>C 20%</text><text x='50' y='110' class='tag ink'>D 15%</text><text x='270' y='50' class='lbl ink'>B + C</text><text x='270' y='70' class='sub mut'>= 25% + 20%</text><text x='270' y='90' class='sub mut'>= 45%</text><rect x='265' y='100' width='120' height='50' rx='6' class='hi'/><text x='275' y='120' class='lbl ink'>45% of</text><text x='275' y='140' class='lbl ink'>1,10,000</text><text x='265' y='165' class='big ink'>49,500</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Pie chart with four slices A=40%, B=25% (highlighted), C=20% (highlighted), D=15%; callout showing B+C = 45% of 1,10,000 = 49,500

given.aim: Find the combined valid vote count for candidates B and C

terms.meaning: All votes deposited in the election including invalid ones
terms.connects: Subtracting invalid votes gives the valid vote pool used for percentages

terms.meaning: Votes that cannot be counted (spoiled, blank, or marked incorrectly)
Each candidate's slice of the valid-vote pie chart
terms.connects: Adding both percentages gives 45%, directly applied to valid votes

given.plan: Compute valid votes = total - invalid; then multiply the combined share of B and C (25% + 20% = 45%) by valid votes.

steps.title: Find total valid votes
steps.formula_raw: Valid votes = Total votes cast - Invalid votes
steps.note: The pie chart percentages apply only to valid votes, not total votes cast.

steps.title: Add B and C percentage shares
steps.note: Summing percentages before multiplying reduces one arithmetic step.

steps.title: Compute combined valid votes for B and C

solution.result: Total valid votes received by B and C together = 49,500 (Option B)

questions.formula_note: No standard combinatorial formula is needed; the solution uses the basic percent-of-a-total calculation applied to the valid vote pool.

questions.question:
The two pie-charts given below show the data of total students and only girls registered in different streams in a university. If the total number of students registered in the university is 5000 and the total number of the registered girls is 1500, then the ratio of boys enrolled in Arts to the girls enrolled in Management is:

Total Students Pie Chart: Arts 28%, Engineering 30%, Science 12%, Commerce 20%, Management 10%
Girls Pie Chart: Arts 20%, Engineering 15%, Science 10%, Commerce 25%, Management 30%

A. 2 : 1
B. 9 : 22
C. 11 : 9
D. 22 : 9
questions.to_find: ratio of boys enrolled in Arts to girls enrolled in Management

understand.plain: Two pie charts give percentage distributions for total students and for girls separately. To find the number of boys in Arts, we subtract the number of girls in Arts from total students in Arts. The girls in Management figure comes directly from the girls pie chart. We then form the ratio.

keywords.explain: Boys in a stream = (stream % in total students pie) x total students MINUS (stream % in girls pie) x total girls.
Read directly from the girls-only pie chart and multiply by total girls count.
Divide both numbers by their GCD to express the ratio in simplest form.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 440 300' width='440' height='300'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='440' height='300' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)' rx='10'/><text x='10' y='25' class='lbl ink'>Total Students (5000)</text><text x='230' y='25' class='lbl ink'>Girls (1500)</text><rect x='10' y='35' width='200' height='160' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='20' y='58' class='sub mut'>Arts: 28% = 1400</text><text x='20' y='78' class='sub mut'>Engg: 30% = 1500</text><text x='20' y='98' class='sub mut'>Science: 12% = 600</text><text x='20' y='118' class='sub mut'>Commerce: 20% = 1000</text><text x='20' y='138' class='sub mut'>Management: 10% = 500</text><text x='20' y='180' class='tag ok'>Arts total = 1400</text><rect x='225' y='35' width='200' height='160' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='235' y='58' class='sub mut'>Arts: 20% = 300</text><text x='235' y='78' class='sub mut'>Engg: 15% = 225</text><text x='235' y='98' class='sub mut'>Science: 10% = 150</text><text x='235' y='118' class='sub mut'>Commerce: 25% = 375</text><text x='235' y='138' class='sub mut'>Management: 30% = 450</text><text x='235' y='180' class='tag no'>Girls Mgmt = 450</text><rect x='10' y='215' width='415' height='70' rx='8' class='hi'/><text x='20' y='240' class='lbl ink'>Boys Arts = 1400 - 300 = 1100</text><text x='20' y='265' class='lbl ink'>Ratio = 1100 : 450 = 22 : 9</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Two side-by-side tables showing stream-wise breakdown for total students and girls; Arts total = 1400 and Girls Management = 450 highlighted; ratio box shows 1100:450 = 22:9

given.aim: Find the ratio of boys in Arts to girls in Management

terms.meaning: The full student population across all streams and genders
terms.connects: Used with the total-students pie chart to get absolute counts per stream

terms.meaning: The number of female students across all streams
terms.connects: Used with the girls-only pie chart to get girls count per stream; boys = total - girls

terms.term: Boys = total students - girls in same stream
terms.meaning: Since boys and girls together make up total students, boys in any stream = total in that stream minus girls in that stream
terms.connects: Links the two pie charts to isolate the male count in each stream

given.plan: Step 1: Use the total-students pie chart to find the absolute count for Arts. Step 2: Use the girls pie chart to find girls in Arts. Subtract to get boys in Arts. Step 3: Use the girls pie chart to find girls in Management. Step 4: Form the ratio and simplify.

steps.title: Find total students in Arts
steps.note: The 28% figure comes from the total-students pie chart for the Arts stream.

steps.title: Find girls in Arts
steps.note: The 20% figure comes from the girls-only pie chart for the Arts stream.

steps.title: Find boys in Arts
steps.formula_raw: Boys in stream = Total in stream - Girls in stream
steps.note: Boys and girls together account for all students in each stream.

steps.title: Find girls in Management
steps.note: The 30% figure comes from the girls-only pie chart for the Management stream.

steps.title: Form the ratio and simplify
steps.note: Dividing both values by 50 gives the simplest form 22:9, matching option D.

solution.result: Ratio of boys enrolled in Arts to girls enrolled in Management = 22 : 9 (Option D)

questions.formula_note: The solution uses the basic percent-of-total calculation three times and the complement rule once to isolate boys in Arts; no advanced combinatorial formulas are required.

formulas.name: Sum of Percentage Shares
formulas.plain: Combined share = p1 + p2 + ... (add percentages, then apply to total)

formulas.name: Deriving Valid Count from Given Data
formulas.plain: Valid = Total given - Excluded (e.g., invalid votes, absent members)
